title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Strings in JavaScript are immutable — every method returns a new string.

Essential string methods:
• .length — number of characters.
• .toUpperCase() / .toLowerCase() — case conversion.
• .trim() / .trimStart() / .trimEnd() — remove whitespace.
• .includes(str) — boolean substring check.
• .startsWith(str) / .endsWith(str).
• .indexOf(str) / .lastIndexOf(str) — position of substring (-1 if not found).
• .slice(start, end) — extract substring.
• .split(separator) — split into array.
• .replace(search, replacement) / .replaceAll().
• .padStart(len, char) / .padEnd(len, char).
• .repeat(n).
• .charAt(i) / [i] — character at index.
• Template literals for concatenation.
